---
Title: Scale of Effects Analysis
Author: Isabelle LT
Created: 2024-10-20
Inputs: ../Data/merged_covariates.csv, ../Data/richness.csv, ../Data/Climate GDD and PET.csv, ../Data/Landscape metrics scaled.rds
Outputs: Optimal scale-of-effect results for each landscape metric
Description: Fits negative binomial GLMMs across a grid of response distances and landscape extents to identify the scale of effect for each landscape configuration metric. For each combination, the focal metric's coefficient, p-value, R-squared, and AIC are extracted. Optimal scales are then selected based on statistical significance.
---

```{R Set up environment}
library(MuMIn)
library(mgcv)
library(glmmTMB)
library(tidyverse)
library(DHARMa)
source("utils.R")
```

```{R Load Data}
survey_year_df <- read_csv(file = "../Data/survey_year.csv")

richness <- read_csv(file = "../Data/richness_dist.csv")

climate_variables <- read.csv(file = "../Data/Climate GDD.csv")

climate_variables_scaled <- climate_variables %>%
  mutate(across(c(pet:gdd), ~ scale(., center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)))

landscape_metrics_all <- readRDS("../Data/Landscape metrics scaled.rds")
```


```{R Flexible modeling framework}
analyze_scale_effects <- function(richness_data,
                                  landscape_data,
                                  focal_metric,
                                  extent_increments = 2,
                                  response_distances = seq(50, 350, by = 25),
                                  survey_year_df, climate_df,
                                  control_vars = TRUE) {
  results_list <- list()
  models_list <- list()

  for (resp_dist in response_distances) {
    rich_filtered <- filter_richness_data(resp_dist, richness_data)

    landscape_focal_data <- landscape_data %>%
      dplyr::select(location, distance_int, focal_metric, upland_prop)

    landscape_metrics_df <- land_metrics_single_obs_multi_landscape_extents(
      landscape_focal_data,
      resp_dist,
      rich_filtered,
      survey_year_df, climate_variables_scaled
    )

    extents <- sort(unique(landscape_metrics_df$distance_int))
    extents_nth <- extents[seq(1, length(extents), by = extent_increments)]

    models_list[[as.character(resp_dist)]] <- list()
    extent_results <- data.frame()

    for (extent in extents_nth) {
      current_extent_data <- landscape_metrics_df %>%
        dplyr::filter(distance_int == extent)

      current_extent_data$survey_year <- as.factor(current_extent_data$survey_year)
      current_extent_data_coords <- current_extent_data %>%
        inner_join(survey_year_df %>%
          select(location, latitude, longitude), by = "location")

      tryCatch(
        {
          formula_string <- paste("richness ~", focal_metric, "+ upland_prop + gdd")

          model <- glmmTMB(as.formula(formula_string),
            data = current_extent_data_coords, family = nbinom2,
            control = glmmTMBControl(optimizer = optim, optArgs = list(method = "BFGS"))
          )

          coef_data <- summary(model)$coefficients

          focal_coef <- coef_data$cond[focal_metric, ]

          extent_results <- rbind(extent_results, data.frame(
            response_distance = resp_dist,
            landscape_extent = extent,
            estimate = focal_coef[1],
            std_error = focal_coef[2],
            t_value = focal_coef[3],
            p_value = focal_coef[4],
            r_squared = suppressWarnings(r.squaredGLMM(model)["trigamma", "R2m"]),
            aic = AIC(model)
          ))
        },
        error = function(e) {
          print(paste("Error occurred for extent", extent, ":", e$message))
        }
      )
    }
    results_list[[as.character(resp_dist)]] <- extent_results
    models_list[[as.character(resp_dist)]] <- model
  }

  return(list(
    results = results_list,
    models = models_list
  ))
}

# Function to identify optimal scales
find_optimal_scales <- function(scale_results,
                                criterion = c("significance", "r_squared", "aic"),
                                alpha = 0.1) {
  optimal_scales <- data.frame()

  for (resp_dist in names(scale_results$results)) {
    results <- scale_results$results[resp_dist][[resp_dist]]

    if (criterion == "significance") {
      # Get all significant scales and sort by effect size
      significant_scales <- results[results$p_value < alpha, ]

      if (nrow(significant_scales) > 0) {
        significant_scales <- significant_scales[order(abs(significant_scales$estimate), decreasing = TRUE), ]
        optimal_scales <- rbind(optimal_scales, significant_scales)
      } else {
        # If none significant, take strongest effect
        optimal <- results[which.max(abs(results$estimate)), ]
        optimal_scales <- rbind(optimal_scales, optimal)
      }
    } else if (criterion == "r_squared") {
      optimal <- results[which.max(results$r_squared), ]
      optimal_scales <- rbind(optimal_scales, optimal)
    } else if (criterion == "aic") {
      optimal <- results[which.min(results$aic), ]
      optimal_scales <- rbind(optimal_scales, optimal)
    }
  }

  return(optimal_scales)
}
```

```{R Fit models}
# Run analysis
analysis_results_metric <- analyze_scale_effects(
  richness_data = richness,
  landscape_data = landscape_metrics_all,
  focal_metric = "cai_cv",
  extent_increment = 5,
  response_distances = seq(50, 350, by = 25),
  survey_year_df = survey_year_df,
  climate_df = climate_variables_scaled,
  control_vars = TRUE
)

# Check residuals
simulateResiduals(analysis_results_metric$model[["225"]], plot = T)

optimal_metric <- find_optimal_scales(analysis_results_metric, criterion = "significance", alpha = 0.1)
```
